coaching.title: Monday Easy Run
coaching.message: 81 days out — you're deep in Peak Phase. Monday is for flushing the legs, not adding fatigue. Target 45–55 min at Zone 1–2 (HR under 140 bpm), fully conversational. If you're on trails, even better — ankle stability and terrain feel are Julian Alps currency. Keep cadence up, let the effort dictate the pace, and stop the moment anything feels off. Note: calendar was unavailable today so this defaults to phase + day-of-week logic.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Post-weekend recovery window is still open. Prioritize glycogen restock today: target 280–320g carbs (elevated above rest-day baseline to support the easy run and ongoing tissue repair). Lean protein at every meal — 30–40g per sitting — for muscle synthesis. Add anti-inflammatory foods: berries, leafy greens, wild salmon or sardines if accessible. Hydrate aggressively — travel + altitude + training compresses recovery if you're dehydrated.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Post-run: 10 minutes of targeted mobility — hip flexors, calves, IT band, and glute medius. Foam roll quads and calves slowly, 60–90 sec per side. If your legs feel heavy from the weekend, elevate for 15 min while doing nasal breathing. Tonight's non-negotiable: 8+ hours of sleep. Peak phase adaptation happens at night — protect that window like a key workout.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: 11.5 weeks to Kranjska Gora. The fitness you're building now is the fitness you'll run on in September. Easy Mondays are not junk miles — they're the connective tissue of a big block. Every controlled effort today keeps Thursday's quality session honest. The Julian Alps don't care how hard you trained; they care how well you recovered. Own this day.
